title: A unified equation for saturation magnetization and spin transport in weakly disordered ferromagnets
abstract: In this report, a unified description of the loss of saturation magnetization in the presence of a distribution of finite-size effects is provided for weakly disordered spin-1/2 ferromagnets. This description allows us to derive a unified form of the Bloch equation for these systems. We further extend this approach to obtain a unified expression for spin transport in such systems.
summary: This paper presents a unified theoretical framework to describe how weak disorder and finite-size effects influence magnetization and spin transport in Heisenberg ferromagnets. It reveals that the resulting spin current follows a temperature dependence similar to the Efros-Shklovskii law found in electronic systems. The findings provide a generalized Bloch equation applicable across different dimensions.
main_problem: The paper aims to provide a unified description of the loss of saturation magnetization and the behavior of spin transport in weakly disordered spin-1/2 ferromagnets.
main_result: The author derives a unified Bloch equation for magnetization and a spin current expression that is structurally analogous to the Efros-Shklovskii law for electronic conductivity.
method: The approach combines the probability distribution of segment lengths with the energy gap dependence on length to derive a gap distribution, using a modified Bloch equation approach.
model_or_system: The system consists of weakly disordered spin-1/2 Heisenberg ferromagnets in 1D, 2D, or 3D, incorporating finite-size effects and a distribution of energy gaps due to disorder.
key_observables: Saturation magnetization (Ms), magnetization loss (Mloss), and spin current (j).
important_parameters: Disorder strength (a), gap scaling constant (c), effective dimensionality (psi), temperature (T), and external magnetic field (H).
main_assumptions: Assumes a weak-disorder and weak-magnetic-field regime, and assumes the magnon group velocity is independent of the magnon population.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the dependence of the mean gap on disorder and dimensionality parameters; Figure 2 shows the variation of saturation magnetization with respect to disorder, scaling, and dimensionality at different temperatures.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of magnetization loss in disordered systems, develops a mathematical framework for gap distributions, derives a unified Bloch equation, extends the derivation to spin transport, and validates the results through parameter variation analysis.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The variation of < ϵm > for different values of the parameters a, c, and ψ is shown.

FIG. 2. (a)–(c) The calculated variation of the saturation magnetization (Ms), obtained by varying the parameters a, c, and ψ, respectively, at different temperatures and in a fixed low magnetic field, is shown. (d) shows representative fits of ln(Mloss) using Equation 11 with F(H) = 1.

FIG. 3. (a) shows the typical values of the exponents α and β obtained from fitting the Mloss data using Equation 11 for various values of < ϵm >, obtained through controlled variation of the parameters a, c, and ψ. (b) A typical variation of Ms as a function of magnetic field is shown. (c) A representative fit of ln(Mloss(H)) obtained using Equation 12 is shown.
